name: Experience Points
description: A numeric progression resource earned from play and accumulated toward levels, unlocks, or reward thresholds.
design_notes: Experience points are only useful when their sources, curve, level thresholds, and reward grants are coherent. XP should define xp_source_id, xp_curve_id, level_threshold_table, overflow behavior, and anti-farm rules so players trust that effort turns into progress.
design_purpose: Reward repeated play with visible long-term growth.
Pace level-ups, unlocks, and skill point grants through a tunable curve.
Give designers a flexible reward amount that can connect combat, quests, exploration, and objectives.
player_fantasy: The player feels that effort is accumulating into durable growth and future options.

parameters.name: xp_curve_id
parameters.description: Identifier for the threshold curve or table used by this progression track.

parameters.name: xp_source_id
parameters.description: Source identifier logged with XP grants for balance and exploit auditing.

parameters.name: level_threshold_table
parameters.description: Ordered XP totals required for each level or milestone.

parameters.name: xp_overflow_policy
parameters.description: Handling for XP earned after the current cap or track limit.

parameters.name: anti_farm_decay_window
parameters.description: Window used to reduce or reject repeated XP from the same source when farming must be controlled.

edge_cases: One reward crosses multiple level_threshold_table entries and must grant every level reward exactly once.
XP is awarded while the player is at cap and later the cap increases.
A quest reward is retried after disconnect and must not duplicate xp_source_id grants.
Balance patches change xp_curve_id and need migration for partial progress.
common_bugs: The UI increments XP optimistically but save data only stores the old value after a crash.
Multiple-level gains grant only the first skill point or unlock because the loop stops after one threshold.
XP is stored as a floating point value and threshold comparisons fail near exact boundaries.
Server and client use different level_threshold_table versions.
balancing_notes: Tune XP sources against expected session length and desired unlock cadence, not only enemy difficulty.
Large one-time rewards need overflow handling so they do not waste player effort at caps.
Anti-farm rules should be visible enough that players do not think rewards randomly stopped.
accessibility_notes: Show current XP, next threshold, and reward preview in readable text.
Avoid progress bars with no numeric context for players who need precise planning information.

implementation_notes.unity: Keep XP grants as integer transactions with xp_source_id and xp_curve_id; process threshold crossing in a loop that grants all level rewards before saving progression_state.
implementation_notes.godot: Store level_threshold_table in a Resource or JSON file and update xp_wallet through a single progression service so combat, quests, and UI cannot apply different rules.
implementation_notes.unreal: Run XP authority in PlayerState or a progression subsystem; replicated clients should receive committed XP totals and level-up reward payloads from the server.
implementation_notes.web: Use integer XP totals and pure functions for threshold crossing; persist grant transaction IDs so page refresh or reconnect cannot replay quest XP.

relationships.reason: Experience points accumulate toward level-up thresholds and progression rewards.
XP or level gains can grant points spent in a skill tree.
XP bars and cooldown displays can compete for HUD attention if both are always visible.

example_games.title: Final Fantasy
example_games.note: Uses experience points to advance character levels in many entries.

example_games.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
example_games.note: Uses XP rewards from quests and encounters toward level progression.

example_games.title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
example_games.note: Uses skill and character progression tied to repeated play.

example_games.title: Dark Souls
example_games.note: Uses earned souls as a visible progression resource for leveling decisions.
